item.title: Bhikkhus, sensual pleasures are impermanent, empty, false, of a delusive nature. Bhikkhus, this is the empty chatter of a fool built on falsehood. Present sensual…
item.description:
Bhikkhus, sensual pleasures are impermanent, empty, false, of a delusive nature. Bhikkhus, this is the empty chatter of a fool built on falsehood. Present sensual pleasures and future sensual pleasures, present perceptions of sensual pleasure and future perceptions of sensual pleasure, both belong to Māra's realm, are Māra's domain, Māra's bait, Māra's feeding ground.

From: Majjhima Nikāya 106 – Āneñjasappāyasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: He avoids wrong livelihood such as: using magical rites for peace, purification rituals, bathing rituals, sacrifice rituals, making oneself vomit, purging, expelling impurities ... treating…
item.description:
He avoids wrong livelihood such as: using magical rites for peace, purification rituals, bathing rituals, sacrifice rituals, making oneself vomit, purging, expelling impurities ... treating eye diseases, practicing medicine, surgery on children, administering root medicines, neutralizing the effects of medicines.

From: Dīgha Nikāya 2 – Sāmaññaphalasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Elder Somamitta: Like climbing a small plank on the ocean, one sinks; So too, one of good conduct sinks when associating with others. Therefore avoid…
item.description:
Elder Somamitta:

Like climbing a small plank on the ocean, one sinks;
So too, one of good conduct sinks when associating with others.
Therefore avoid the lazy, the indolent.

Live near the noble ones, the secluded, the energetic,
Meditative, always striving, the wise and clear-sighted.

From: Theragāthā 2.14
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Elder Bakula: One who first intends to do something later — That person destroys his own basis of happiness, Later he experiences suffering in burning…
item.description:
Elder Bakula:

One who first intends to do something later —
That person destroys his own basis of happiness,
Later he experiences suffering in burning regret.

Speak only of what you do, not of what you don't.
The wise know the one who only speaks but does not act.

Nibbāna is wonderfully blissful, taught by the Perfectly Enlightened One,
Without sorrow, without greed, truly secure.
There, suffering is completely destroyed.

From: Theragāthā 3.3
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: - This saṃsāra is without discoverable beginning, bhikkhus. A first point is not discerned of beings roaming and wandering on, shrouded by ignorance and fettered…
item.description:
- This saṃsāra is without discoverable beginning, bhikkhus. A first point is not discerned of beings roaming and wandering on, shrouded by ignorance and fettered by craving. What do you think, bhikkhus, which is greater: the stream of blood that has flowed from your cut heads as you roamed and wandered on this long journey, or the water in the four great oceans? This is greater, bhikkhus, the stream of blood that has flowed from your cut heads as you roamed and wandered on this long journey — not the water in the four great oceans. Bhikkhus, the stream of blood that has flowed from your cut heads when you were born as cows and roamed for a long time as cows... when you were born as sheep... as goats... as deer... as fowl... as pigs... Bhikkhus, the stream of blood that has flowed from your cut heads when you were bandits, village thieves caught for a long time... when you were bandits, adulterers caught for a long time — that stream is greater, not the water in the four great oceans.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 15.13
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Bhikkhus, a bhikkhu who thinks: 'Covetousness and unrighteous greed are defilements of the mind' and, knowing thus, abandons covetousness and unrighteous greed, the defilements of…
item.description:
Bhikkhus, a bhikkhu who thinks: 'Covetousness and unrighteous greed are defilements of the mind' and, knowing thus, abandons covetousness and unrighteous greed, the defilements of the mind; thinks: 'Hatred is a defilement of the mind' and, knowing thus, abandons hatred, the defilement of the mind; anger ... resentment ... hypocrisy ... malice, envy, stinginess, deceit, fraud ... obstinacy ... impetuosity ... conceit ... arrogance ... vanity; thinks: 'Heedlessness is a defilement of the mind' and, knowing thus, abandons heedlessness, the defilement of the mind.

From: Majjhima Nikāya 7 – Vatthasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: There are these five dangers, bhikkhus, in living too long in one place. What five? Many belongings, storing up many belongings; many medicines, storing up…
item.description:
There are these five dangers, bhikkhus, in living too long in one place. What five? Many belongings, storing up many belongings; many medicines, storing up many medicines; many duties, many things to do, not being skilled in what needs to be done; living in close association with laypeople and renunciants, indulging in lay connections; and when leaving that dwelling, one leaves with regret. Bhikkhus, these are the five dangers in living too long in one place.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 5.223
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Cundī, whatever kind of virtue there is, the virtues loved by the noble ones are called the foremost among those virtues, that is, virtue that…
item.description:
Cundī, whatever kind of virtue there is, the virtues loved by the noble ones are called the foremost among those virtues, that is, virtue that is unbroken, unblemished, unstained, untainted, leading to liberation, praised by the wise, not clung to, leading to right concentration. Those who are complete in the virtues loved by the noble ones are complete in the foremost. Those who have confidence in the foremost have the foremost result.

Confidence in the foremost,
Knowing the foremost Dhamma,
Confidence in the Buddha, foremost,
Worthy of respect, unsurpassed.

Confidence in the Dhamma, foremost,
Dispassion, peace, bliss,
Confidence in the Saṅgha, foremost,
The unsurpassed field of merit.

Giving to the foremost,
Foremost merit increases,
Foremost in life span,
Complexion and fame,
Foremost in happiness,
Foremost in strength.

The wise give to the foremost,
Established in foremost Dhamma,
Whether devas or humans,
Attain foremost joy.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 5.32
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unwholesome states, the bhikkhu enters and dwells in the first jhāna, which is accompanied by thought and examination,…
item.description:
Quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unwholesome states, the bhikkhu enters and dwells in the first jhāna, which is accompanied by thought and examination, with rapture and ease born of seclusion. He drenches, saturates, fills, and suffuses his body with the rapture and ease born of seclusion, so that no part of his entire body is not suffused with that rapture.

From: Dīgha Nikāya 2 – Sāmaññaphalasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Elder Nun Vimalā, formerly a courtesan: Infatuated with my caste, With my beauty and youth, With fame and renown, I relied on my youth, Despising…
item.description:
Elder Nun Vimalā, formerly a courtesan: Infatuated with my caste, With my beauty and youth, With fame and renown, I relied on my youth, Despising others, Looking down on them. I adorned this body, I seduced fools, I stood at the door, At the shop of beauty, Like a cruel hunter Setting his trap. I boasted of my adornments, Revealed many hidden parts, To mock many men, I performed many tricks. Today, going for alms, Shaven-headed, wearing the great robe, I sit at the foot of a tree, I attain thought-free concentration. All bonds are destroyed, Both divine and human bonds. Having abandoned all taints, I am cooled, at peace.

From: Therīgāthā 5.2
📖 Read this sutta
